obligations.obligation:
NLC has claimed that it availed of the benefit of tax holiday under Section 80 IA
of the IT Act with effect from the year 2007-08 and passed on the benefit to KSEB as
mandated by the regulations.

In view of these findings, the question whether NLC was obligated to avail
the benefit of Section 80 IA from the date of commissioning of TPS-I Expansion does
not survive for our examination.

During the course of hearing it was submitted on behalf of NLC that even though
the tax benefit has already been passed on by NLCIL to the beneficiaries, the
Order in Petition No. 2/MP/2014 (on remand) Page 2 of 34assessing officer in his assessment order dated 28.12.2010 has disallowed its claim
fo...

In case, NLC becomes liable to
pay income-tax on account of unavailability of benefit under Section 80 IA, it shall be
entitled to recover from KSEBL the income-tax along surcharge, interest etc paid to
the Income-tax Department.

We direct that KSEB shall release such
withheld income-tax dues amounting to 57.00 crore pertaining to TPS-II along with
interest at the rate of 9% per annum from June 2007 within 30 days upon NLC
furnishing the claim, duly supported by the statutory auditors' certificate.

We direct that KSEB shall release such
withheld income-tax dues amounting to `57.00 crore pertaining to TPS-II along with
interest at the rate of 9% per annum from June 2007 within 30 days upon NLC
furnishing the claim, duly supported by the statutory auditors’ certificate.”
2.

Even if the Appellant has availed tax benefit under Section 80-I A in respect of
TPS-I Expansion from FY 2007-08, there was no taxable income for TPS-I
Expansion during 2004-05 to 2006-07.

Pursuant to the above APTEL judgment, the liability of the Respondent
beneficiaries to reimburse the tax, as per the provisions of Regulation 7 of the 2024
Tariff Regulations, was to be determined, taking in into account the applicability of the
tax holiday, provided under Section 80IA of the Income...

In this
backdrop, NLCIL has filed the present Petition under Section 79(1)(f) of the Electricity
Act, 2003 seeking (i) adjudication of the disputes raised by KSEBL with regard to the
implementation of the Commission’s order and upheld by APTEL vide its judgment
dated 3.7.2013; and (ii) a declaration...

In view of the above, in our opinion the points raised by KSEB and other
respondents do not warrant any intervention as the same is in line with APTEL
judgement dated 03.07.2013 in Appeal No. 250/2012 Accordingly, the parties should
act in accordance with Revised IT reimbursement claim duly certifie...

The needful shall
be done by the Commission within two months from the date of this order positively.

Section 80 IA Deduction
d) Tariff in relation to PSA is governed by the 2001 and 2004 Tariff Regulations.

As per proviso 3 to Regulation 7(2), the benefit of a tax holiday as applicable
under the IT Act shall be passed on to beneficiaries.

Without prejudice to this,
from COD, for any consecutive period of 10 years (out of the first 15 years),
NLCIL’s TPS-I Expansion Project and all directly related industrial activities are
entitled to a tax holiday amounting to 100% of the profit and gains derived from
such business under Section 80 ...

Under clause 4 of this section,
the various categories of business eligible to the same have been identified under
which an undertaking set up for the generation of power has been included.

It is
evident from the language of Section 80IA, that it is the undertaking itself that is
eligible for a complete tax holiday and not various sub-businesses carried out by
Order in Petition No. 2/MP/2014 (on remand) Page 6 of 34the undertaking.

NLCIL having been set up for power generations is an
undertaking eligible for the benefits provided under Section 80IA.

e) No manner of reading of the provision indicates any such bar on mining
activities of NLCIL being barred from availing tax deductions under Section 80IA
of the IT Act.

f) The fact that the mining activity of NLCIL is eligible for Section 80IA deduction
stands confirmed by the Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) vide its order
dated 11.4.2012 in I.T.A. Nos.

Thus, the issue that NLCIL is eligible
to avail the deductions under Section 80IA stands settled.

It
becomes incumbent upon NLCIL to diligently prosecute any tax holiday
disallowance in the light of the statutory advantage contained in the 2001 Tariff
Regulations viz, the benefit of any applicable tax holiday shall be passed on to
the beneficiaries as also the observations of this Commission in ...

Core Business
h) Without prejudice to the above, in the event that there is bar placed on the
mining activity under Section 80IA, this itself is a testimony to the fact that mining
activity does not form core business and thus no tax liability for the same can be
passed on to the beneficiaries.

Regulation 7(1) of the 2004 Tariff Regulations
prescribe that tax on income streams of the generating company, only from its
core business can be recovered from the beneficiaries.

Further, the first Proviso
to Regulation 7(2) specifically stipulates that tax on any income stream other than
‘core business’ shall not constitute a pass-through component in tariff and tax on
such other income shall be payable by the generating company.

Res Judicata and Estoppel
l) NLCIL’s contention that tax holiday under Section 80 IA is not available to
mines and thus, KSEBL is liable to reimburse the income tax paid on the linked
mining activities of NLCIL, already been raised by it in the earlier round of
proceedings.
